title: iris reticulata subsp. kurdica
intro: Iris reticulata subsp. kurdica, also known as the Kurdish iris, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the iris family. It is a perennial plant with purple flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Iris reticulata subsp. kurdica include infusion, decoction, and tincture, which can be used to make tea, poultice, or topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
